info.title: Remove date prefix from plan filename generation

diffs.before:
package app

import (
	"context"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/charmbracelet/bubbles/spinner"
	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config"
	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstate"
	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/session"
	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/ui"
	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/ui/overlay"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// TestSoloAgent_NoAutomaticPushPromptOnExit verifies that when a solo agent's
// tmux session exits, the automatic push-then-review flow does NOT trigger.
func TestSoloAgent_NoAutomaticPushPromptOnExit(t *testing.T) {
	const planFile = "2026-02-25-solo-test.md"

	dir := t.TempDir()
	plansDir := filepath.Join(dir, "docs", "plans")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(plansDir, 0o755))

	_, ps, fsm := newSharedStoreForTest(t, plansDir)
	require.NoError(t, ps.Register(planFile, "solo test", "plan/solo-test", time.Now()))
	seedPlanStatus(t, ps, planFile, planstate.StatusImplementing)

	inst, err := session.NewInstance(session.InstanceOptions{
		Title:     "solo-test-solo",
		Path:      t.TempDir(),
		Program:   "claude",
		PlanFile:  planFile,
		AgentType: session.AgentTypeCoder,
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)
	inst.SoloAgent = true

	sp := spinner.New(spinner.WithSpinner(spinner.Dot))
	list := ui.NewNavigationPanel(&sp)
	_ = list.AddInstance(inst)

	h := &home{
		ctx:               context.Background(),
		state:             stateDefault,
		appConfig:         config.DefaultConfig(),
		nav:               list,
		menu:              ui.NewMenu(),
		tabbedWindow:      ui.NewTabbedWindow(ui.NewPreviewPane(), ui.NewDiffPane(), ui.NewInfoPane()),
		toastManager:      overlay.NewToastManager(&sp),
		planState:         ps,
		planStateDir:      plansDir,
		fsm:               fsm,
		waveOrchestrators: make(map[string]*WaveOrchestrator),
	}

	msg := metadataResultMsg{
		Results: []instanceMetadata{
			{Title: inst.Title, TmuxAlive: false},
		},
		PlanState: ps,
	}

	model, _ := h.Update(msg)
	updated, ok := model.(*home)
	require.True(t, ok)

	assert.NotEqual(t, stateConfirm, updated.state,
		"solo agent exit must NOT trigger confirmation overlay")
	assert.Nil(t, updated.confirmationOverlay,
		"solo agent exit must NOT set confirmation overlay")
}

diffs.before:
package app

import (
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/internal/clickup"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestImportClickUpTask_WritesScaffold(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	plansDir := filepath.Join(dir, "docs", "plans")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(plansDir, 0o755))

	task := &clickup.Task{
		ID:          "abc123",
		Name:        "Design Auth Flow",
		Description: "Implement OAuth2 for the API gateway",
		Status:      "In Progress",
		URL:         "https://app.clickup.com/t/abc123",
		Subtasks: []clickup.Subtask{
			{Name: "Add login endpoint", Status: "open"},
			{Name: "Add token refresh", Status: "open"},
		},
	}

	scaffold := clickup.ScaffoldPlan(*task)
	filename := clickup.ScaffoldFilename(task.Name, "2026-02-24")
	planPath := filepath.Join(plansDir, filename)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(planPath, []byte(scaffold), 0o644))

	// Verify file exists and contains expected content
	data, err := os.ReadFile(planPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	content := string(data)
	assert.Contains(t, content, "**Goal:** Implement OAuth2")
	assert.Contains(t, content, "**Source:** ClickUp abc123")
	assert.Contains(t, content, "- [ ] Add login endpoint")
	assert.Contains(t, content, "- [ ] Add token refresh")
}

func TestScaffoldFilename_Dedup(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	base := clickup.ScaffoldFilename("Test Task", "2026-02-24")

	// No collision keeps original filename.
	assert.Equal(t, base, dedupePlanFilename(dir, base))

	// First collision returns -2 suffix.
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, base), []byte("x"), 0o644))
	name2 := dedupePlanFilename(dir, base)
	assert.Equal(t, "2026-02-24-test-task-2.md", name2)

	// Second collision returns -3 suffix.
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, name2), []byte("x"), 0o644))
	assert.Equal(t, "2026-02-24-test-task-3.md", dedupePlanFilename(dir, base))
}

package planfsm

import (
	"net/http/httptest"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"testing"

	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstore"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestScanSignals_ParsesValidSentinels(t *testing.T) {
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".kasmos", "signals")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755))

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
		filepath.Join(signalsDir, "planner-finished-2026-02-22-foo.md"),
		nil, 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
		filepath.Join(signalsDir, "review-changes-2026-02-22-bar.md"),
		[]byte("fix the tests"), 0o644))

	signals := ScanSignals(signalsDir)
	require.Len(t, signals, 2)

	// Sort by plan file for deterministic assertion
	if signals[0].PlanFile > signals[1].PlanFile {
		signals[0], signals[1] = signals[1], signals[0]
	}

	assert.Equal(t, PlannerFinished, signals[1].Event)
	assert.Equal(t, "2026-02-22-foo.md", signals[1].PlanFile)
	assert.Empty(t, signals[1].Body)

	assert.Equal(t, ReviewChangesRequested, signals[0].Event)
	assert.Equal(t, "2026-02-22-bar.md", signals[0].PlanFile)
	assert.Equal(t, "fix the tests", signals[0].Body)
}

func TestScanSignals_IgnoresInvalidFiles(t *testing.T) {
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".kasmos", "signals")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755))

	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
		filepath.Join(signalsDir, "garbage-file.txt"),
		nil, 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
		filepath.Join(signalsDir, ".hidden"),
		nil, 0o644))

	signals := ScanSignals(signalsDir)
	assert.Empty(t, signals)
}

func TestScanSignals_EmptyDirReturnsNil(t *testing.T) {
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".kasmos", "signals")
	signals := ScanSignals(signalsDir)
	assert.Nil(t, signals)
}

func TestScanSignals_RejectsUserOnlyEvents(t *testing.T) {
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".kasmos", "signals")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755))

	// An agent trying to drop a cancel sentinel — should be ignored
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
		filepath.Join(signalsDir, "cancel-2026-02-22-foo.md"),
		nil, 0o644))

	signals := ScanSignals(signalsDir)
	assert.Empty(t, signals)
}

func TestSignalKey_Dedup(t *testing.T) {
	a := Signal{Event: ReviewChangesRequested, PlanFile: "foo.md"}
	b := Signal{Event: ReviewChangesRequested, PlanFile: "foo.md"}
	c := Signal{Event: ImplementFinished, PlanFile: "foo.md"}

	assert.Equal(t, a.Key(), b.Key(), "same event+planFile should produce same key")
	assert.NotEqual(t, a.Key(), c.Key(), "different events should produce different keys")
}

func TestConsumeSignal_DeletesFile(t *testing.T) {
	dir := t.TempDir()
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".signals")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755))

	path := filepath.Join(signalsDir, "planner-finished-test.md")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, nil, 0o644))

	sig := Signal{Event: PlannerFinished, PlanFile: "test.md", filePath: path}
	ConsumeSignal(sig)

	_, err := os.Stat(path)
	assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err))
}

func TestScanSignals_KasmosSignalsDir(t *testing.T) {
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".kasmos", "signals")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(
		filepath.Join(signalsDir, "planner-finished-test.md"),
		[]byte(""), 0o644,
	))

	signals := ScanSignals(signalsDir)
	require.Len(t, signals, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, PlannerFinished, signals[0].Event)
}

// TestSignals_WithStoreFSM verifies that signals still trigger store-backed FSM transitions.
// The sentinel file system is decoupled from storage — it just triggers FSM events.
func TestSignals_WithStoreFSM(t *testing.T) {
	backend := planstore.NewTestSQLiteStore(t)
	srv := httptest.NewServer(planstore.NewHandler(backend))
	defer srv.Close()

	store := planstore.NewHTTPStore(srv.URL, "test-project")
	err := store.Create("test-project", planstore.PlanEntry{
		Filename: "test.md", Status: "planning",
	})
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Write a sentinel file in a temp signals dir
	signalsDir := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), ".kasmos", "signals")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(signalsDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(signalsDir, "planner-finished-test.md"), nil, 0o644))

	signals := ScanSignals(signalsDir)
	require.Len(t, signals, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, PlannerFinished, signals[0].Event)

	// Apply via store-backed FSM
	fsm := New(store, "test-project", signalsDir)
	require.NoError(t, fsm.Transition("test.md", signals[0].Event))

	entry, err := store.Get("test-project", "test.md")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "ready", string(entry.Status))
}

diffs.before:
package planstore_test

import (
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/kastheco/kasmos/config/planstore"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newTestStore(t *testing.T) planstore.Store {
	t.Helper()
	store, err := planstore.NewSQLiteStore(":memory:")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	t.Cleanup(func() { store.Close() })
	return store
}

func TestSQLiteStore_CreateAndGet(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)
	entry := planstore.PlanEntry{
		Filename:    "2026-02-28-test-plan.md",
		Status:      planstore.StatusReady,
		Description: "test plan",
		Branch:      "plan/test-plan",
		CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("kasmos", entry))

	got, err := store.Get("kasmos", "2026-02-28-test-plan.md")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, planstore.StatusReady, got.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, "test plan", got.Description)
}

func TestSQLiteStore_ListByStatus(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("kasmos", planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "a.md", Status: planstore.StatusReady}))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("kasmos", planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "b.md", Status: planstore.StatusDone}))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("kasmos", planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "c.md", Status: planstore.StatusReady}))

	plans, err := store.ListByStatus("kasmos", planstore.StatusReady)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, plans, 2)
}

func TestSQLiteStore_ProjectIsolation(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("project-a", planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "x.md", Status: planstore.StatusReady}))
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("project-b", planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "y.md", Status: planstore.StatusReady}))

	plans, err := store.List("project-a")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, plans, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "x.md", plans[0].Filename)
}

func TestSQLiteStore_Update(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)
	entry := planstore.PlanEntry{
		Filename:    "2026-02-28-update-test.md",
		Status:      planstore.StatusReady,
		Description: "original description",
		Branch:      "plan/update-test",
		CreatedAt:   time.Now().UTC(),
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("kasmos", entry))

	entry.Status = planstore.StatusImplementing
	entry.Description = "updated description"
	require.NoError(t, store.Update("kasmos", "2026-02-28-update-test.md", entry))

	got, err := store.Get("kasmos", "2026-02-28-update-test.md")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, planstore.StatusImplementing, got.Status)
	assert.Equal(t, "updated description", got.Description)
}

func TestSQLiteStore_Rename(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)

func TestClickUpTaskIDRoundTrip(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)
	entry := planstore.PlanEntry{Filename: "2026-02-28-clickup-test.md", Status: planstore.StatusReady}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create("proj", entry))

	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "", got.ClickUpTaskID, "task ID must be empty before set")

	// Set the task ID
	require.NoError(t, store.SetClickUpTaskID("proj", "2026-02-28-clickup-test.md", "CU-abc123"))

	// Verify it round-trips through Get
	got, err = store.Get("proj", "2026-02-28-clickup-test.md")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "CU-abc123", got.ClickUpTaskID, "task ID must be persisted after SetClickUpTaskID")

	// Verify it appears in List
	plans, err := store.List("proj")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, plans, 1)
	assert.Equal(t, "CU-abc123", plans[0].ClickUpTaskID, "task ID must appear in List results")
}

func TestClickUpTaskIDRoundTrip_NotFound(t *testing.T) {
	store := newTestStore(t)
	err := store.SetClickUpTaskID("proj", "nonexistent.md", "CU-xyz")
	require.Error(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "not found")
}

parts.text:
Implement Task 1: Remove date prefix from filename generation, DisplayName, Rename, and all tests

Load the `kasmos-coder` skill before starting. Also load `cli-tools` for the tool selection reference.

## Plan Context

**Goal:** Stop prepending `YYYY-MM-DD-` to plan filenames so that working on a plan across multiple days doesn't cause slug mismatches; the slug alone is a sufficient unique identifier.
**Architecture:** The date prefix is generated in 4 places: `buildPlanFilename()`, `createPlanEntry()`, `importClickUpTask()`, and `ScaffoldFilename()`. `DisplayName()` strips it for display, and `Rename()` preserves it. All of these must be updated to produce dateless slugs (e.g. `auth-refactor.md` instead of `2026-02-21-auth-refactor.md`). `DisplayName()` becomes a simple `.md` strip. `Rename()` drops the date-preservation logic. The planner skill template, planner agent prompt, and ~20 test files need their hardcoded `2026-XX-XX-` prefixed filenames updated. The `dedupePlanFilename` functions already work on the base slug so they need no logic changes, only test expectation updates.
**Tech Stack:** Go, bubbletea (TUI), planstate/planstore packages, kasmos-planner skill (Markdown)

## Wave 1 of 2

## Task Instructions

**Files:**
- Modify: `config/planstate/planstate.go`
- Modify: `internal/clickup/scaffold.go`
- Modify: `app/app_state.go`
- Modify: `cmd/plan.go`
- Modify: `session/git/plan_lifecycle.go`
- Test: `config/planstate/planstate_test.go`
- Test: `internal/clickup/scaffold_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_plan_creation_test.go`
- Test: `app/clickup_import_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_plan_actions_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_plan_completion_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_plan_context_actions_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_planner_signal_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_solo_agent_test.go`
- Test: `app/app_wave_orchestration_flow_test.go`
- Test: `app/clickup_progress_test.go`
- Test: `app/plan_cancel_rename_delay_test.go`
- Test: `cmd/plan_test.go`
- Test: `config/planfsm/signals_test.go`
- Test: `config/planfsm/wave_signal_test.go`
- Test: `config/planstore/http_test.go`
- Test: `config/planstore/migrate_test.go`
- Test: `config/planstore/sqlite_test.go`
- Test: `session/git/plan_lifecycle_test.go`
- Test: `session/instance_planfile_test.go`
- Test: `session/instance_title_test.go`

**Step 1: write the failing test**

Update `TestBuildPlanFilename` in `app/app_plan_creation_test.go`:
```go
func TestBuildPlanFilename(t *testing.T) {
	got := buildPlanFilename("Auth Refactor", time.Date(2026, 2, 21, 10, 0, 0, 0, time.UTC))
	want := "auth-refactor.md"
	if got != want {
		t.Fatalf("buildPlanFilename() = %q, want %q", got, want)
	}
}
```

Update `TestScaffoldFilename` in `internal/clickup/scaffold_test.go` — remove date arg and update expectations:
```go
func TestScaffoldFilename(t *testing.T) {
	tests := map[string]string{
		"Design Auth Flow":       "design-auth-flow.md",
		"API v2 — New Endpoints": "api-v2-new-endpoints.md",
		"  spaces & symbols!!! ": "spaces-symbols.md",
	}
	for input, want := range tests {
		got := clickup.ScaffoldFilename(input)
		assert.Equal(t, want, got, "input: %q", input)
	}
}
```

**Step 2: run test to verify it fails**

```bash
go test ./app/... -run TestBuildPlanFilename -v
go test ./internal/clickup/... -run TestScaffoldFilename -v
```

expected: FAIL — signature mismatch (`ScaffoldFilename` now takes 1 arg) and wrong output

**Step 3: write minimal implementation**

**Production code changes:**

1. **`config/planstate/planstate.go`** — simplify `DisplayName` to just strip `.md`:
```go
// DisplayName strips the .md extension from a plan filename.
// "auth-refactor.md" → "auth-refactor"
func DisplayName(filename string) string {
	return strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
}
```

2. **`config/planstate/planstate.go`** — simplify `Rename` to drop date-prefix preservation. Remove the `if len(oldFilename) > 11 && ...` block and always use `newSlug + ".md"`:
```go
var newFilename string
newFilename = newSlug + ".md"
```

3. **`app/app_state.go`** — update `buildPlanFilename` to drop date:
```go
// buildPlanFilename derives the plan filename from a human name.
// "Auth Refactor" → "auth-refactor.md"
func buildPlanFilename(name string, _ time.Time) string {
	slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
	if slug == "" {
		slug = "plan"
	}
	return slug + ".md"
}
```

4. **`app/app_state.go`** — update `createPlanEntry` to drop date from filename:
```go
slug := slugifyPlanName(name)
filename := slug + ".md"
```

5. **`app/app_state.go`** — update `importClickUpTask` to call `ScaffoldFilename` without date. Remove the `date := time.Now().Format("2006-01-02")` line and change the call to `clickup.ScaffoldFilename(task.Name)`.

6. **`internal/clickup/scaffold.go`** — remove date parameter from `ScaffoldFilename`:
```go
// ScaffoldFilename generates a plan filename from a task name.
func ScaffoldFilename(name string) string {
	slug := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
	slug = nonAlphanumeric.ReplaceAllString(slug, "-")
	slug = strings.Trim(slug, "-")
	return slug + ".md"
}
```

7. **`cmd/plan.go`** — update `executePlanRegister` branch derivation to drop date-stripping:
```go
if branch == "" {
	slug := strings.TrimSuffix(planFile, ".md")
	branch = "plan/" + slug
}
```

8. **`session/git/plan_lifecycle.go`** — update doc comment:
```go
// PlanBranchFromFile derives the git branch name from a plan filename.
// "auth-refactor.md" → "plan/auth-refactor"
```

**Test fixture updates (use `sd` for batch replacements):**

Use `rg` to find all `"2026-\d{2}-\d{2}-` prefixed plan filenames in test files, then use `sd` to strip the date prefix. Key patterns:

- `sd '"2026-\d{2}-\d{2}-([^"]+)"' '"$1"'` across all `_test.go` files — strips date prefix from string literals
- Update `ScaffoldFilename` call sites in tests to remove the date argument
- Update `dedupePlanFilename` test expectations (e.g. `"test-task-2.md"` not `"2026-02-24-test-task-2.md"`)
- Update `Rename` test expectations (no longer preserves date prefix)
- Update `DisplayName` test expectations (now just strips `.md`)

**Step 4: run test to verify it passes**

```bash
go test ./... -count=1
```

expected: PASS — all tests compile and pass with dateless slugs

**Step 5: commit**

```bash
git add -A
git commit -m "feat: remove date prefix from plan slug generation, display, and all tests"
```

state.output:
<skill_content name="kasmos-coder">
# Skill: kasmos-coder

# kasmos-coder

You implement code. One task (managed) or a full plan sequentially (manual). Your disciplines are TDD, systematic debugging, evidence-first verification, and safe shared-worktree hygiene. Review feedback gets technical evaluation, not performative agreement.

---

## CLI Tools Hard Gate

<HARD-GATE>
### Banned Tools

These legacy tools are NEVER permitted. Using them is a violation, not a preference.

| Banned | Replacement | No Exceptions |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `grep` | `rg` (ripgrep) | Even for simple one-liners |
| `grep -r` | `rg` | Recursive grep is still grep |
| `grep -E` | `rg` | Extended regex is still grep |
| `sed` | `sd` | Even for one-liners |
| `awk` | `yq`/`jq` (structured) or `sd` (text) | No awk for any purpose |
| `find` | `fd` or glob tools | Even for simple file listing |
| `diff` (standalone) | `difft` | `git diff` is fine — standalone `diff` is not |
| `wc -l` | `scc` | Even for single files |

**`git diff` is allowed** — it's a git subcommand, not standalone `diff`.

**STOP.** If you are about to type `grep`, `sed`, `awk`, `find`, `diff`, or `wc` — stop. There are no exceptions.
</HARD-GATE>

### Tool Selection

| Task | Use | Not |
|------|-----|-----|
| Find literal string | `rg` | `grep` |
| Find files by name/ext | `fd` | `find` |
| Replace string in files | `sd` | `sed` |
| Rename symbol across files | `ast-grep` | `sed`/`sd` |
| Structural code rewrite | `comby` | `sed`/`awk` |
| Find code pattern | `ast-grep --pattern` | `rg` |
| Read/modify YAML/TOML/JSON | `yq` / `jq` | `sed`/`awk` |
| Review code changes | `difft` | `diff` |
| Spell check code | `typos` | manual |
| Count lines / codebase metrics | `scc` | `wc -l` |

---

## Where You Fit

### Env Vars

| Variable | Meaning |
|----------|---------|
| `KASMOS_MANAGED` | Set to `1` when kasmos TUI spawned you. Unset in raw terminal. |
| `KASMOS_TASK` | The task number you're assigned (managed only). |
| `KASMOS_WAVE` | The wave number (managed only). |
| `KASMOS_PEERS` | Number of sibling agents running in parallel. `0` or unset = solo. |

### Managed (`KASMOS_MANAGED=1`)

kasmos spawned you to implement **one specific task** identified by `KASMOS_TASK`. Your scope:

1. Read the plan content from the plan store (via `kas plan` CLI or the plan content API). Find your wave (`KASMOS_WAVE`) and task (`KASMOS_TASK`).
2. Implement that single task following TDD discipline below.
3. Commit your work with task number in the commit message.
4. **Stop.** Do not implement other tasks — they belong to sibling agents or future waves.

**Do NOT write the implement-finished sentinel.** kasmos detects task completion via prompt
detection (when your agent returns to its input prompt) and handles the
implementing→reviewing transition through the wave orchestrator. Writing implement-finished
from a task agent prematurely triggers review and breaks wave orchestration.

### Manual (KASMOS_MANAGED unset)

You execute the full plan sequentially, wave by wave:

1. Read the plan. Check its **Size** field to determine wave structure.
2. Create a todo list with one item per task (all `pending`), then begin.
3. Execute one wave at a time: implement each task (TDD), commit, then proceed to next task.
4. After each wave completes, self-review before starting the next wave.
5. When all waves complete, write the implement-finished sentinel (see **Signaling**).

---

## TDD Discipline

### The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Wrote code before writing the test? Delete it. Start over from the test. No exceptions:
- Don't keep it "for reference" — you'll adapt it, which is testing after
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests — delete means delete
- Implement fresh from tests

### RED → GREEN → REFACTOR

**RED — Write the failing test**

Write one minimal test showing the required behavior. Run it. Confirm:
- Test actually fails (not errors from syntax — a genuine test failure)
- Failure message is the expected one ("function not found", "assertion error")
- Test fails because the feature is missing, not a typo

**Never skip the RED verification.** If the test passes immediately, you are testing existing behavior. Fix the test.

**GREEN — Minimal implementation**

Write the simplest code that makes the test pass. Don't add features. Don't refactor other code. Don't "improve" beyond what the test requires. Verify:
- Your test passes
- All other tests still pass
- No new errors or warnings

**REFACTOR — Clean up**

Only after green: remove duplication, improve names, extract helpers. Never add behavior during refactor. Keep tests green throughout.

**Repeat** — one test per behavior. When the task is fully implemented, all new tests are green and no existing tests are broken.

### Red Flags — STOP and Start Over

| Thought | Action |
|---------|--------|
| "I'll write tests after to verify it works" | Delete code, write test first |
| "Too simple to need a test" | Write the test — it takes 30 seconds |
| "I already manually tested it" | Manual ≠ systematic. Write the test |
| "I'll keep this code as reference" | Delete it. Keeping = adapting = testing after |
| "TDD will slow me down" | Debugging later is slower. Write the test |
| "Just this once" | Not a valid exception |

---

## Shared Worktree Safety

When `KASMOS_PEERS > 0`, sibling agents are modifying this worktree concurrently.

**Never:**
- `git add .` or `git add -A` — you will stage a sibling's in-progress files
- `git stash` — you will destroy sibling's uncommitted changes
- `git reset` — you will destroy sibling's uncommitted changes
- `git checkout -- <file>` on files you didn't modify — you will revert a sibling's edits
- Run project-wide formatters (`go fmt ./...`, `prettier --write .`) — scope to your files only
- Run project-wide linters across files you didn't touch

**Always:**
- `git add <specific-files>` — only the files you changed
- Include task number in every commit message: `feat(task-6): add kasmos-coder skill`
- Expect untracked files and dirty state from siblings — ignore them
- Run tests scoped to your changed packages where possible before committing

**When you see test failures in files outside your task scope:** Do not attempt to fix them. They may be caused by incomplete parallel work from a sibling agent. Report the failure context in your signal and stop.

---

## Debugging Discipline

When tests fail, builds break, or behavior is unexpected — investigate before proposing fixes. Random patches mask the actual problem and create new ones.

### The Four Phases

**Phase 1 — Root Cause Investigation**

Before attempting any fix:
1. Read error messages completely — stack traces, line numbers, error codes
2. Reproduce the failure consistently — what exact steps trigger it?
3. Check recent changes — git diff, recent commits, env differences
4. In multi-component systems: add diagnostic instrumentation at each boundary to find exactly where it breaks. Run once to gather evidence, then analyze.
5. Trace data flow — where does the bad value originate? Trace backward up the call stack to the source.

**Phase 2 — Pattern Analysis**

Find working examples in the codebase doing something similar. Compare them against the broken code. List every difference — don't assume "that can't matter." Understand all dependencies (config, env, state).

**Phase 3 — Hypothesis and Testing**

Form one specific hypothesis: "I think X is the root cause because Y." Test it with the smallest possible change. One variable at a time. Verify — did it work? If not, form a new hypothesis. Don't stack fixes.

**Phase 4 — Implementation**

Write a failing test reproducing the bug first (TDD discipline applies to bugfixes). Implement the single fix addressing the root cause. Verify the test now passes and no other tests regressed.

**If the fix doesn't work:** Return to Phase 1. Re-analyze with new information.

**If 3 fixes have failed:** STOP. Question the architecture — each fix revealing a new problem in a different place is an architectural signal, not a debugging problem. Escalate or document the situation rather than attempting fix #4.

### Red Flags — Return to Phase 1

- "Quick fix for now, investigate later"
- "Just try changing X and see if it works"
- "It's probably X, let me fix that"
- "I don't fully understand but this might work"
- Proposing solutions before tracing data flow
- "One more fix attempt" (when you've already tried 2+)
- Each fix reveals a new problem in a different place

---

## Verification

Before claiming a task is complete, before committing, before writing the finished sentinel:

### The Gate

```
1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this works?
2. RUN: Execute it fresh. Complete output.
3. READ: Full output. Check exit code. Count failures.
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence.
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence.
5. ONLY THEN: Claim completion.
```

Skipping any step is not verification — it's guessing.

### Common Verification Commands

```bash
# Go
go build ./...
go test ./...
go test ./path/to/package/...   # scoped — preferred in shared worktree

# Node
npm test
npm run build

# Python
pytest
pytest path/to/tests/

# General
go vet ./...
typos                            # spell check before committing
```

### Red Flags — STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to" before verification
- Claiming tests pass without running them in this message
- Trusting previous run output — always run fresh
- Expressing satisfaction ("Done!", "Perfect!") before the verification command output is in view
- Committing without a clean build

---

## Handling Reviewer Feedback

Code review requires technical evaluation, not performative agreement.

### Response Pattern

```
1. READ: Complete feedback without reacting
2. UNDERSTAND: Restate the technical requirement (or ask if unclear)
3. VERIFY: Check against the actual codebase
4. EVALUATE: Is this technically sound for THIS codebase?
5. RESPOND: Technical acknowledgment, or reasoned pushback with evidence
6. IMPLEMENT: One item at a time, test each
```

### Forbidden Responses

**Never:**
- "You're absolutely right!" — performative
- "Great point!" / "Excellent feedback!" — performative
- "Let me implement that now" — before verification

**Instead:**
- Restate the technical requirement
- Ask for clarification if anything is unclear
- Push back with technical reasoning if the suggestion is wrong
- Just start working — actions over words

### Before Implementing Any Item

- Unclear about any item? **Stop. Ask for clarification on all unclear items first.** Do not implement partial feedback — items may be related.
- Does it break existing functionality? Verify before proceeding.
- Does it violate YAGNI? Check if the feature is actually used (`rg` in codebase).
- Does it conflict with architectural decisions already made? Escalate rather than blindly implement.

### When to Push Back

Push back when:
- The suggestion breaks existing functionality (show the test)
- The reviewer lacks full context for the decision
- It violates YAGNI (feature not used anywhere)
- It's technically incorrect for this stack/version
- It conflicts with established architectural decisions

**How:** Technical reasoning, not defensiveness. Reference working tests. Reference the code.

### Implementation Order

For multi-item feedback:
1. Clarify all unclear items first
2. Blocking issues (breaks, security holes)
3. Simple fixes (typos, wrong identifiers, missing imports)
4. Complex fixes (refactoring, logic changes)
5. Test each fix individually before moving to the next
6. Verify no regressions after all items complete

### Acknowledging Correct Feedback

```
✅ "Fixed. [One sentence describing the change]"
✅ "Good catch — [specific issue]. Fixed in [location]."
✅ [Just fix it. The code shows you heard the feedback.]

❌ "You're absolutely right!"
❌ "Thanks for catching that!"
❌ Any gratitude expression
```

---

## Signaling

### Managed (`KASMOS_MANAGED=1`)

**Do NOT write any sentinel files.** kasmos detects task completion automatically when your
agent returns to its input prompt. The wave orchestrator handles all lifecycle transitions
(implementing→reviewing) and reviewer spawning.

After implementing and committing your task: **stop.** Do not implement other tasks, do not
write signal files, do not invoke branch finishing — kasmos handles orchestration.

**Do not modify plan state directly.**

### Manual (KASMOS_MANAGED unset)

Execute waves sequentially:

1. Implement all tasks in Wave 1 (TDD, commit each task)
2. Self-review Wave 1 before proceeding — run full tests, check for regressions
3. Implement Wave 2, self-review, continue through all waves
4. After all waves complete and all tests pass:

```bash
# Transition the plan to "reviewing" via CLI
kas plan transition <plan-file> request_review
```

Then handle branch finishing — present these options to the user:

```
implementation complete. what would you like to do?

1. merge back to <base-branch> locally
2. push and create a pull request
3. keep the branch as-is (handle it later)
4. discard this work

which option?
```

**Option 1 — Merge locally (worktree-safe):**
```bash
# First, rebase onto latest main so the merge is a fast-forward
git fetch origin <base-branch>
git rebase origin/<base-branch>
<run tests on rebased result>

# Merge from the ROOT worktree (that's where <base-branch> is checked out)
ROOT_WORKTREE="$(git worktree list --porcelain | head -1 | sd '^worktree ' '')"
git -C "$ROOT_WORKTREE" merge <feature-branch>

# Clean up
git worktree remove <worktree-path>
git branch -d <feature-branch>
```
> **Why not `git checkout <base-branch>` here?** In a worktree layout the base branch is
> already checked out in the root worktree. Git prevents a branch from being checked out
> in two worktrees simultaneously. Instead, run the merge *from* the root worktree using
> `git -C`.

**Option 2 — Push and create PR:**
```bash
git push -u origin <feature-branch>
gh pr create --title "<title>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## summary
- <what changed>
- <why>

## test plan
- [ ] <verification steps>
EOF
)"
```
Keep the worktree until the PR merges.

**Option 3 — Keep as-is:** Report path. Don't clean up. Don't update plan state.

**Option 4 — Discard:** Confirm first with exact text `discard`. Then:
```bash
# Remove worktree first (must leave it before deleting), then delete the branch
git worktree remove <worktree-path>
git branch -D <feature-branch>
```

Update plan status to `done` after options 1, 2, or 4:
```bash
kas plan set-status <plan-file> done --force
```

---

## Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Fix |
|---------|-----|
| Writing production code before a failing test | Delete the code, write the test first |
| Claiming tests pass without running them | Run the command, read the output |
| `git add .` in a shared worktree | `git add <specific-files>` only |
| Attempting fix #4 after 3 have failed | Stop — it's architecture, not a bug |
| Performative agreement with reviewer | Technical verification, then act |
| Implementing unclear review feedback | Ask for clarification on ALL unclear items first |
| Running project-wide formatters | Scope formatters to your changed files only |
| Modifying plan state directly when `KASMOS_MANAGED=1` | Write sentinel file instead — kasmos handles state |
| Implementing sibling tasks in managed mode | Implement ONE task (KASMOS_TASK), then signal and stop |

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/kas/dev/kasmos/.worktrees/plan-remove-date-from-plan-slugs/.agents/skills/kasmos-coder
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

</skill_files>
</skill_content>

<skill_content name="cli-tools">
# Skill: cli-tools

# CLI Tools

Modern CLI tools that replace legacy unix utilities for code-related tasks. These tools understand syntax, structure, and context — producing safer, faster, and more accurate results than regex-based alternatives.

<HARD-GATE>
## Banned Tools

These legacy tools are NEVER permitted. Using them is a violation, not a preference.

| Banned | Replacement | No Exceptions |
|--------|-------------|---------------|
| `grep` | `rg` (ripgrep) | Even for simple one-liners. `rg` is faster, respects .gitignore, and handles encoding correctly |
| `grep -r` | `rg` | Recursive grep is still grep. Always `rg` |
| `grep -E` | `rg` | Extended regex is still grep. `rg` supports the same patterns |
| `sed` | `sd` | Even for one-liners. `sd` has saner syntax and no delimiter escaping |
| `awk` | `yq`/`jq` (structured) or `sd` (text) | No awk for any purpose |
| `find` | `fd` or glob tools | Even for simple file listing. `fd` respects .gitignore; use `fd -e go` for extension |
| `diff` (standalone) | `difft` | `git diff` is fine — standalone `diff` is not |
| `wc -l` | `scc` | Even for single files |

**`git diff` is allowed** — it's a git subcommand, not standalone `diff`. Use `GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git diff` when reviewing code changes.

**STOP.** If you are about to type `grep`, `sed`, `awk`, `find`, `diff`, or `wc` — stop and use the replacement. There are no exceptions. "Just this once" is a violation.
</HARD-GATE>

<HARD-GATE>
## Batch Edit Rule

When you need to make the **same or similar change across multiple files**, you MUST use a CLI tool (`sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep`) instead of repeated Edit tool calls. This is not a suggestion.

| Change type | Tool | Example |
|-------------|------|---------|
| Literal string replacement across files | `sd` | `sd 'oldFunc' 'newFunc' src/**/*.go` |
| Same structural pattern across files | `comby` | `comby 'before(:[args])' 'after(:[args])' .go -in-place` |
| Symbol rename across files | `ast-grep` | `ast-grep run --pattern 'old($$$)' --rewrite 'new($$$)' --lang go` |
| Same line deletion across files | `sd` | `sd 'lineToRemove\n' '' src/**/*.go` |

**The threshold is 3.** If you are about to make the same Edit in 3+ files, stop and use a CLI tool instead. One command replaces N edit calls — it's faster, less error-prone, and produces smaller diffs.

**Red flags** — if you catch yourself doing any of these, you are violating this rule:
- Copy-pasting the same `oldString`/`newString` into multiple Edit calls
- Making a list of files to edit one-by-one with the same pattern
- "I'll just do this manually, it's only N files" (if N ≥ 3, use a tool)
- Using Edit with `replaceAll: true` across multiple files for the same string

**STOP.** If you are about to make the same edit in 3+ files — stop and use `sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep`. There are no exceptions.
</HARD-GATE>

## Tool Selection by Task

Pick the right tool for the job. When multiple tools could work, the table shows the preferred choice and why.

| Task | Use | Not | Why |
|------|-----|-----|-----|
| Rename symbol across files | `ast-grep` | `sed`/`sd` | AST-aware, won't rename inside strings/comments |
| Structural code rewrite | `comby` | `sed`/`awk` | Understands balanced delimiters, nesting |
| Find code pattern | `ast-grep --pattern` | `grep`/`rg` | Matches syntax, not text |
| Find literal string | `rg` | `grep` | Fast, respects .gitignore, correct encoding |
| Find files by name/extension | `fd` | `find` | Respects .gitignore, simpler syntax |
| Replace string in files | `sd` | `sed` | No delimiter escaping, sane defaults |
| Read/modify YAML/TOML/JSON | `yq` / `jq` | `sed`/`awk` | Understands structure, preserves formatting |
| Review code changes | `difft` | `diff` | Syntax-aware, ignores formatting noise |
| Spell check code | `typos` | manual | Understands camelCase, identifiers |
| Count lines / codebase metrics | `scc` | `wc -l`/`cloc` | Fast, includes complexity estimates |
| Multi-line structural rewrite | `comby` | `sed` | Handles cross-line patterns with holes |
| Add/change function params | `comby` | manual | `func F(:[params])` patterns |
| Simple literal find-replace | `sd` | `sed` | Simpler syntax, in-place by default |

## ast-grep vs comby Decision

Both do structural code operations. Choose based on the transformation:

| Scenario | Winner | Reason |
|----------|--------|--------|
| Rename all calls to a function | ast-grep | AST-level symbol matching |
| Find all usages of a pattern | ast-grep | Tree-sitter precision |
| Add/change function parameters | comby | Hole syntax `:[params]` is natural |
| Replace entire function body | comby | `{:[_]}` discards balanced block |
| Insert code after a function | comby | Match + append in rewrite |
| Add to import block | comby | `import (:[imports])` pattern |
| Multi-line structural rewrite | comby | Balanced delimiter matching |
| Lint-style pattern detection | ast-grep | Rule-based scanning |

## Quick Reference

### rg (ripgrep)

Fast text search that respects .gitignore. Primary replacement for all `grep` variants.

```bash
rg 'pattern' [path]                            # basic search (recursive by default)
rg 'pattern' --type go                         # filter by language type
rg 'pattern' -g '*.go'                         # filter by glob
rg 'pattern' -g '*.go' -g '!*_test.go'         # include/exclude globs
rg -i 'pattern'                                # case insensitive
rg -F 'literal[0]'                             # fixed string (no regex)
rg -U 'start\n.*end'                           # multiline match
rg -l 'pattern'                                # list matching files only
rg -c 'pattern'                                # count matches per file
rg --no-heading -n 'pattern'                   # machine-readable output (file:line:match)
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/rg.md](resources/rg.md)

### ast-grep (`ast-grep`)

AST-based code search and replace using tree-sitter. Matches syntax structure, not text.

```bash
ast-grep run --pattern 'fmt.Errorf($$$)' --lang go          # find all calls
ast-grep run --pattern 'errors.New($A)' --rewrite 'fmt.Errorf($A)' --lang go  # replace
ast-grep run --pattern '$A != nil' --lang go src/            # search specific path
```

Key patterns: `$VAR` (single node), `$$$` (variable-length args), `$_` (wildcard)

**In-depth reference:** [resources/ast-grep.md](resources/ast-grep.md)

### comby (`comby`)

Structural search/replace that understands balanced delimiters, strings, and comments. Superior to regex for multi-line code transformations.

```bash
comby 'func :[name](:[args]) {:[body]}' 'func :[name](:[args]) error {:[body]}' .go -in-place
comby 'import (:[imports])' 'import (:[imports]
	"new/package"
)' file.go -in-place
```

**Critical rule:** Always use `{:[body]}` (inline braces), never split `{` and `}` on separate lines.

Key holes: `:[var]` (everything), `:[[var]]` (word), `:[_]` (unnamed wildcard), `:[var\n]` (to newline)

**In-depth reference:** [resources/comby.md](resources/comby.md) — read this before any non-trivial comby usage. Contains critical pitfalls around whitespace normalization and balanced delimiter matching.

### difftastic (`difft`)

Syntax-aware structural diff. Dramatically cleaner output for refactors and moves.

```bash
difft old.go new.go                              # compare files
GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git diff                  # git integration
GIT_EXTERNAL_DIFF=difft git log -p -- path/file   # file history
difft --display inline old.go new.go              # single-column output
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/difftastic.md](resources/difftastic.md)

### sd (`sd`)

Modern sed alternative. In-place by default, no delimiter escaping needed.

```bash
sd 'old_name' 'new_name' src/**/*.go              # replace in files
sd 'version = "(\d+)"' 'version = "2"' file.toml  # regex with groups
sd -p 'foo' 'bar' file.txt                         # preview (dry run)
sd -F 'literal[0]' 'replacement' file.txt          # fixed string mode
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/sd.md](resources/sd.md)

### yq (`yq`)

YAML/JSON processor (jq wrapper for YAML). Query and modify structured config files.

```bash
yq '.metadata.name' file.yaml                     # read field
yq -y '.key = "value"' file.yaml                   # modify YAML
yq -y '.dependencies += {"new": "^1.0"}' pkg.yaml  # add to map
cat file.yaml | yq '.items[].name'                 # query array
```

Note: This is the Python-based `yq` (kislyuk/yq), a jq wrapper. Uses jq filter syntax. Use `-y` flag for YAML output, otherwise outputs JSON.

**In-depth reference:** [resources/yq.md](resources/yq.md)

### typos (`typos`)

Fast source code spell checker. Understands identifiers, camelCase, filenames.

```bash
typos                          # check entire project
typos src/                     # check specific path
typos --write-changes          # auto-fix typos
typos --format brief           # compact output
```

Run before commits to catch typos in identifiers, strings, and comments.

**In-depth reference:** [resources/typos.md](resources/typos.md)

### scc (`scc`)

Fast code counter with complexity estimates. Useful for project scope assessment.

```bash
scc                                  # full project summary
scc internal/                        # specific directory
scc --by-file --sort lines           # per-file breakdown
scc --not-match '_test.go$'          # exclude test files
scc --include-ext go,ts              # specific languages only
```

**In-depth reference:** [resources/scc.md](resources/scc.md)

## Violations

These violations are not suggestions. If you do any of these, you are violating the skill.

| Violation | Required Fix |
|-----------|-------------|
| Using `grep` for anything | Use `rg` for text search, `ast-grep` for code patterns |
| Using `grep -r` (recursive) | Use `rg` — recursive by default, also respects .gitignore |
| Using `grep -E` (extended regex) | Use `rg` — supports full regex including extended patterns |
| Using `sed` for anything | Use `sd` for replacements, `ast-grep`/`comby` for refactoring |
| Using `awk` for anything | Use `yq`/`jq` for structured data, `sd` for text processing |
| Using `find` for anything | Use `fd` for file finding, glob tools for path patterns |
| Using standalone `diff` | Use `difft` for syntax-aware structural diffs |
| Using `wc -l` for counting | Use `scc` for language-aware counts + complexity |
| Splitting `{` / `}` in comby templates | Always inline: `{:[body]}` not `{\n:[body]\n}` |
| Forgetting `-in-place` with comby | Without it, comby only previews changes |
| Repeated Edit calls with same pattern across 3+ files | Use `sd`, `comby`, or `ast-grep` — one command, not N edits |
| Using Edit `replaceAll` across multiple files for same string | Use `sd 'old' 'new' file1 file2 ...` or glob pattern |

Base directory for this skill: file:///home/kas/dev/kasmos/.worktrees/plan-remove-date-from-plan-slugs/.agents/skills/cli-tools
Relative paths in this skill (e.g., scripts/, reference/) are relative to this base directory.
Note: file list is sampled.

<skill_files>

32: 	CreatedAt     time.Time `json:"created_at,omitempty"`
33: 	Implemented   string    `json:"implemented,omitempty"`
34: 	ClickUpTaskID string    `json:"clickup_task_id,omitempty"`
35: }
36: 
37: type TopicEntry struct {
38: 	CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
39: }
40: 
41: type PlanState struct {
42: 	Dir          string
43: 	Plans        map[string]PlanEntry
44: 	TopicEntries map[string]TopicEntry
45: 	store        planstore.Store // always non-nil
46: 	project      string          // project name used with the store
47: }
48: 
49: type PlanInfo struct {
50: 	Filename    string
51: 	Status      Status
52: 	Description string
53: 	Branch      string
54: 	Topic       string
55: 	CreatedAt   time.Time
56: }
57: 
58: type TopicInfo struct {
59: 	Name      string
60: 	CreatedAt time.Time
61: }
62: 
63: // Load creates a PlanState backed by the given store. Plans and TopicEntries are
64: // populated from the store. dir is retained for file operations (e.g. Rename moves
65: // the .md file on disk). The store is always required — there is no JSON fallback.
66: func Load(store planstore.Store, project, dir string) (*PlanState, error) {
67: 	plans, err := store.List(project)
68: 	if err != nil {
69: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
70: 	}
71: 
72: 	topics, err := store.ListTopics(project)
73: 	if err != nil {
74: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
75: 	}
76: 
77: 	ps := &PlanState{
78: 		Dir:          dir,
79: 		Plans:        make(map[string]PlanEntry, len(plans)),
80: 		TopicEntries: make(map[string]TopicEntry, len(topics)),
81: 		store:        store,
82: 		project:      project,
83: 	}
84: 
85: 	for _, e := range plans {
86: 		ps.Plans[e.Filename] = PlanEntry{
87: 			Status:        Status(e.Status),
88: 			Description:   e.Description,
89: 			Branch:        e.Branch,
90: 			Topic:         e.Topic,
91: 			CreatedAt:     e.CreatedAt,
92: 			Implemented:   e.Implemented,
93: 			ClickUpTaskID: e.ClickUpTaskID,
94: 		}
95: 	}
96: 
97: 	for _, t := range topics {
98: 		ps.TopicEntries[t.Name] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: t.CreatedAt}
99: 	}
100: 
101: 	return ps, nil
102: }
103: 
104: // Topics returns all topic entries sorted by name.
105: func (ps *PlanState) Topics() []TopicInfo {
106: 	// Discover topics from both TopicEntries and plan topic fields.
107: 	seen := make(map[string]TopicInfo)
108: 	for name, entry := range ps.TopicEntries {
109: 		seen[name] = TopicInfo{Name: name, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt}
110: 	}
111: 	for _, entry := range ps.Plans {
112: 		if entry.Topic != "" {
113: 			if _, ok := seen[entry.Topic]; !ok {
114: 				seen[entry.Topic] = TopicInfo{Name: entry.Topic}
115: 			}
116: 		}
117: 	}
118: 	result := make([]TopicInfo, 0, len(seen))
119: 	for _, info := range seen {
120: 		result = append(result, info)
121: 	}
122: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
123: 		return result[i].Name < result[j].Name
124: 	})
125: 	return result
126: }
127: 
128: // PlansByTopic returns all plans in the given topic, sorted by filename.
129: func (ps *PlanState) PlansByTopic(topic string) []PlanInfo {
130: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
131: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
132: 		if entry.Topic == topic {
133: 			result = append(result, PlanInfo{
134: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
135: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
136: 				Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
137: 			})
138: 		}
139: 	}
140: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
141: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
142: 	})
143: 	return result
144: }
145: 
146: // UngroupedPlans returns all active plans with no topic, sorted by filename.
147: func (ps *PlanState) UngroupedPlans() []PlanInfo {
148: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
149: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
150: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
151: 			continue
152: 		}
153: 		if entry.Topic == "" {
154: 			result = append(result, PlanInfo{
155: 				Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
156: 				Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
157: 				CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
158: 			})
159: 		}
160: 	}
161: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
162: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
163: 	})
164: 	return result
165: }
166: 
167: // hasTopicEntry returns true if the given topic name exists in TopicEntries.
168: func (ps *PlanState) hasTopicEntry(topic string) bool {
169: 	if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
170: 		return false
171: 	}
172: 	_, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]
173: 	return exists
174: }
175: 
176: // HasRunningCoderInTopic checks if any plan in the given topic (other than
177: // excludePlan) has status StatusImplementing. Returns the conflicting plan filename.
178: func (ps *PlanState) HasRunningCoderInTopic(topic, excludePlan string) (bool, string) {
179: 	if topic == "" {
180: 		return false, ""
181: 	}
182: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
183: 		if filename == excludePlan {
184: 			continue
185: 		}
186: 		if entry.Topic == topic && entry.Status == StatusImplementing {
187: 			return true, filename
188: 		}
189: 	}
190: 	return false, ""
191: }
192: 
193: // Unfinished returns plans that are not done or cancelled, sorted by filename.
194: func (ps *PlanState) Unfinished() []PlanInfo {
195: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
196: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
197: 		if entry.Status == StatusDone || entry.Status == StatusCancelled {
198: 			continue
199: 		}
200: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
201: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
202: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
203: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
204: 		})
205: 	}
206: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
207: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
208: 	})
209: 	return result
210: }
211: 
212: // Finished returns plans that are done, sorted by creation time (newest first).
213: func (ps *PlanState) Finished() []PlanInfo {
214: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
215: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
216: 		if entry.Status != StatusDone {
217: 			continue
218: 		}
219: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
220: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
221: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
222: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
223: 		})
224: 	}
225: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
226: 		if !result[i].CreatedAt.Equal(result[j].CreatedAt) {
227: 			return result[i].CreatedAt.After(result[j].CreatedAt)
228: 		}
229: 		return result[i].Filename > result[j].Filename
230: 	})
231: 	return result
232: }
233: 
234: // Cancelled returns all cancelled plans, sorted by filename.
235: func (ps *PlanState) Cancelled() []PlanInfo {
236: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0)
237: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
238: 		if entry.Status != StatusCancelled {
239: 			continue
240: 		}
241: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
242: 			Filename: filename, Status: entry.Status,
243: 			Description: entry.Description, Branch: entry.Branch,
244: 			Topic: entry.Topic, CreatedAt: entry.CreatedAt,
245: 		})
246: 	}
247: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
248: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
249: 	})
250: 	return result
251: }
252: 
253: // List returns all plans (including done and cancelled), sorted by filename.
254: func (ps *PlanState) List() []PlanInfo {
255: 	result := make([]PlanInfo, 0, len(ps.Plans))
256: 	for filename, entry := range ps.Plans {
257: 		result = append(result, PlanInfo{
258: 			Filename:    filename,
259: 			Status:      entry.Status,
260: 			Description: entry.Description,
261: 			Branch:      entry.Branch,
262: 			Topic:       entry.Topic,
263: 			CreatedAt:   entry.CreatedAt,
264: 		})
265: 	}
266: 	sort.Slice(result, func(i, j int) bool {
267: 		return result[i].Filename < result[j].Filename
268: 	})
269: 	return result
270: }
271: 
272: // IsDone returns true only if the given plan has status StatusDone.
273: func (ps *PlanState) IsDone(filename string) bool {
274: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
275: 	if !ok {
276: 		return false
277: 	}
278: 	return entry.Status == StatusDone
279: }
280: 
281: // ForceSetStatus overrides a plan's status regardless of FSM rules.
282: // Validates the status is a known value. Use only for manual overrides (e.g. kq plan set-status --force).
283: func (ps *PlanState) ForceSetStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
284: 	if !isValidStatus(status) {
285: 		return fmt.Errorf("invalid status %q: must be one of ready, planning, implementing, reviewing, done, cancelled", status)
286: 	}
287: 	if _, ok := ps.Plans[filename]; !ok {
288: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
289: 	}
290: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
291: 	entry.Status = status
292: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
293: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
294: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
295: 	}
296: 	return nil
297: }
298: 
299: // isValidStatus returns true if s is a recognised lifecycle status.
300: func isValidStatus(s Status) bool {
301: 	switch s {
302: 	case StatusReady, StatusPlanning, StatusImplementing, StatusReviewing, StatusDone, StatusCancelled:
303: 		return true
304: 	}
305: 	return false
306: }
307: 
308: // setStatus updates a plan's status and persists to the store.
309: // Unexported: only for use within this package (tests). Production code must use planfsm.Transition.
310: func (ps *PlanState) setStatus(filename string, status Status) error {
311: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
312: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
313: 	}
314: 	entry := ps.Plans[filename]
315: 	entry.Status = status
316: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
317: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
318: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
319: 	}
320: 	return nil
321: }
322: 
323: // CreateWithContent adds a new plan entry with markdown content stored in the backend.
324: // The plan entry is created with StatusReady, and the content is persisted via store.SetContent.
325: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
326: func (ps *PlanState) CreateWithContent(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time, content string) error {
327: 	if err := ps.Create(filename, description, branch, topic, createdAt); err != nil {
328: 		return err
329: 	}
330: 	if err := ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content); err != nil {
331: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store set content: %w", err)
332: 	}
333: 	return nil
334: }
335: 
336: // GetContent retrieves the markdown content for the given plan filename from the store.
337: func (ps *PlanState) GetContent(filename string) (string, error) {
338: 	return ps.store.GetContent(ps.project, filename)
339: }
340: 
341: // SetContent updates the markdown content for the given plan filename in the store.
342: func (ps *PlanState) SetContent(filename, content string) error {
343: 	return ps.store.SetContent(ps.project, filename, content)
344: }
345: 
346: // Create adds a new plan entry to the state and auto-creates the topic if needed.
347: func (ps *PlanState) Create(filename, description, branch, topic string, createdAt time.Time) error {
348: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
349: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
350: 	}
351: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
352: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
353: 	}
354: 	entry := PlanEntry{
355: 		Status:      StatusReady,
356: 		Description: description,
357: 		Branch:      branch,
358: 		Topic:       topic,
359: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
360: 	}
361: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
362: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
363: 	if topic != "" {
364: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
365: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
366: 		}
367: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
368: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
369: 		}
370: 	}
371: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
372: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
373: 	}
374: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
375: 	if topic != "" {
376: 		topicEntry := planstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: createdAt.UTC()}
377: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
378: 			// Ignore "already exists" errors for topics
379: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
380: 				return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
381: 			}
382: 		}
383: 	}
384: 	return nil
385: }
386: 
387: // Register adds a new plan entry with metadata and persists to the store.
388: // Returns an error if the plan already exists.
389: func (ps *PlanState) Register(filename, description, branch string, createdAt time.Time) error {
390: 	if ps.Plans == nil {
391: 		ps.Plans = make(map[string]PlanEntry)
392: 	}
393: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[filename]; exists {
394: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan already exists: %s", filename)
395: 	}
396: 	entry := PlanEntry{
397: 		Status:      StatusReady,
398: 		Description: description,
399: 		Branch:      branch,
400: 		CreatedAt:   createdAt.UTC(),
401: 	}
402: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
403: 	if err := ps.store.Create(ps.project, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
404: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
405: 	}
406: 	return nil
407: }
408: 
409: // Entry returns the PlanEntry for the given filename, and whether it exists.
410: func (ps *PlanState) Entry(filename string) (PlanEntry, bool) {
411: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
412: 	return entry, ok
413: }
414: 
415: // SetTopic assigns a topic to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
416: // If topic is non-empty and does not yet exist in TopicEntries, it is auto-created.
417: // Pass an empty string to remove the plan from any topic.
418: func (ps *PlanState) SetTopic(filename, topic string) error {
419: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
420: 	if !ok {
421: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
422: 	}
423: 	entry.Topic = topic
424: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
425: 	// Auto-create topic entry if it doesn't exist
426: 	if topic != "" {
427: 		if ps.TopicEntries == nil {
428: 			ps.TopicEntries = make(map[string]TopicEntry)
429: 		}
430: 		if _, exists := ps.TopicEntries[topic]; !exists {
431: 			ps.TopicEntries[topic] = TopicEntry{CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC()}
432: 		}
433: 	}
434: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
435: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
436: 	}
437: 	// Auto-create topic in store if needed
438: 	if topic != "" {
439: 		topicEntry := planstore.TopicEntry{Name: topic, CreatedAt: ps.TopicEntries[topic].CreatedAt}
440: 		if err := ps.store.CreateTopic(ps.project, topicEntry); err != nil {
441: 			if !isAlreadyExistsError(err) {
442: 				return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
443: 			}
444: 		}
445: 	}
446: 	return nil
447: }
448: 
449: // SetBranch assigns a branch name to an existing plan entry and persists to the store.
450: func (ps *PlanState) SetBranch(filename, branch string) error {
451: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
452: 	if !ok {
453: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
454: 	}
455: 	entry.Branch = branch
456: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
457: 	if err := ps.store.Update(ps.project, filename, ps.toPlanstoreEntry(filename, entry)); err != nil {
458: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
459: 	}
460: 	return nil
461: }
462: 
463: // Save is a no-op — all mutations write through to the store immediately.
464: // Retained for API compatibility.
465: func (ps *PlanState) Save() error {
466: 	return nil
467: }
468: 
469: // DisplayName strips the date prefix and .md extension from a plan filename.
470: // "2026-02-20-my-feature.md" → "my-feature"
471: // "plain-plan.md" → "plain-plan"
472: func DisplayName(filename string) string {
473: 	name := strings.TrimSuffix(filename, ".md")
474: 	if len(name) > 11 && name[4] == '-' && name[7] == '-' && name[10] == '-' {
475: 		name = name[11:]
476: 	}
477: 	return name
478: }
479: 
480: // Rename renames a plan by giving it a new display name slug.
481: // It renames the .md file on disk (if it exists), rekeys the planstate entry,
482: // and persists the updated state. The date prefix of the old filename is preserved.
483: // newName should be a human-readable name (e.g., "auth refactor") which will be
484: // slugified automatically. Returns the new filename on success.
485: func (ps *PlanState) Rename(oldFilename, newName string) (string, error) {
486: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[oldFilename]
487: 	if !ok {
488: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", oldFilename)
489: 	}
490: 	if newName == "" {
491: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name cannot be empty")
492: 	}
493: 
494: 	// Build new filename preserving the date prefix (YYYY-MM-DD-) if present.
495: 	newSlug := slugify(newName)
496: 	if newSlug == "" {
497: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("new name produced an empty slug")
498: 	}
499: 	var newFilename string
500: 	if len(oldFilename) > 11 && oldFilename[4] == '-' && oldFilename[7] == '-' && oldFilename[10] == '-' {
501: 		newFilename = oldFilename[:11] + newSlug + ".md"
502: 	} else {
503: 		newFilename = newSlug + ".md"
504: 	}
505: 
506: 	if newFilename == oldFilename {
507: 		return oldFilename, nil // nothing to do
508: 	}
509: 	if _, exists := ps.Plans[newFilename]; exists {
510: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("a plan named %q already exists", newFilename)
511: 	}
512: 
513: 	// Rename file on disk if it exists.
514: 	oldPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, oldFilename)
515: 	newPath := filepath.Join(ps.Dir, newFilename)
516: 	if _, err := os.Stat(oldPath); err == nil {
517: 		if err := os.Rename(oldPath, newPath); err != nil {
518: 			return "", fmt.Errorf("rename plan file: %w", err)
519: 		}
520: 	}
521: 
522: 	// Rekey the planstate entry.
523: 	ps.Plans[newFilename] = entry
524: 	delete(ps.Plans, oldFilename)
525: 
526: 	if err := ps.store.Rename(ps.project, oldFilename, newFilename); err != nil {
527: 		return "", fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
528: 	}
529: 	return newFilename, nil
530: }
531: 
532: // slugify converts a human name to a lowercase, hyphen-separated slug.
533: // "My Cool Feature!" → "my-cool-feature"
534: func slugify(name string) string {
535: 	name = strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
536: 	// Replace any sequence of non-alphanumeric characters with a hyphen.
537: 	result := make([]rune, 0, len(name))
538: 	inHyphen := false
539: 	for _, r := range name {
540: 		if r >= 'a' && r <= 'z' || r >= '0' && r <= '9' {
541: 			result = append(result, r)
542: 			inHyphen = false
543: 		} else if !inHyphen && len(result) > 0 {
544: 			result = append(result, '-')
545: 			inHyphen = true
546: 		}
547: 	}
548: 	// Trim trailing hyphen.
549: 	for len(result) > 0 && result[len(result)-1] == '-' {
550: 		result = result[:len(result)-1]
551: 	}
552: 	return string(result)
553: }
554: 
555: // isAlreadyExistsError returns true if the error indicates a duplicate resource.
556: func isAlreadyExistsError(err error) bool {
557: 	if err == nil {
558: 		return false
559: 	}
560: 	msg := err.Error()
561: 	return strings.Contains(msg, "already exists")
562: }
563: 
564: // toPlanstoreEntry converts a local PlanEntry to a planstore.PlanEntry for
565: // writing to the store.
566: func (ps *PlanState) toPlanstoreEntry(filename string, e PlanEntry) planstore.PlanEntry {
567: 	return planstore.PlanEntry{
568: 		Filename:      filename,
569: 		Status:        planstore.Status(e.Status),
570: 		Description:   e.Description,
571: 		Branch:        e.Branch,
572: 		Topic:         e.Topic,
573: 		CreatedAt:     e.CreatedAt,
574: 		Implemented:   e.Implemented,
575: 		ClickUpTaskID: e.ClickUpTaskID,
576: 	}
577: }
578: 
579: // SetClickUpTaskID assigns a ClickUp task ID to an existing plan entry and
580: // persists to the store.
581: func (ps *PlanState) SetClickUpTaskID(filename, taskID string) error {
582: 	entry, ok := ps.Plans[filename]
583: 	if !ok {
584: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan not found: %s", filename)
585: 	}
586: 	entry.ClickUpTaskID = taskID
587: 	ps.Plans[filename] = entry
588: 	if err := ps.store.SetClickUpTaskID(ps.project, filename, taskID); err != nil {
589: 		return fmt.Errorf("plan store: %w", err)
590: 	}
591: 	return nil
592: }

11: // ScaffoldPlan generates a plan markdown from a ClickUp task.
12: func ScaffoldPlan(task Task) string {
13: 	var b strings.Builder
14: 
15: 	fmt.Fprintf(&b, "# %s\n\n", task.Name)
16: 
17: 	if task.Description != "" {
18: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "**Goal:** %s\n\n", task.Description)
19: 	}
20: 
21: 	if task.ID != "" {
22: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "**Source:** ClickUp %s", task.ID)
23: 		if task.URL != "" {
24: 			fmt.Fprintf(&b, " (%s)", task.URL)
25: 		}
26: 		b.WriteString("\n\n")
27: 	}
28: 
29: 	if task.Status != "" {
30: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "**ClickUp Status:** %s\n\n", task.Status)
31: 	}
32: 
33: 	if task.Priority != "" {
34: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "**Priority:** %s\n\n", task.Priority)
35: 	}
36: 
37: 	if task.ListName != "" {
38: 		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "**List:** %s\n\n", task.ListName)
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	if len(task.Subtasks) > 0 {
42: 		b.WriteString("## Reference: ClickUp Subtasks\n\n")
43: 		for _, st := range task.Subtasks {
44: 			checkbox := "- [ ] "
45: 			if isDone(st.Status) {
46: 				checkbox = "- [x] "
47: 			}
48: 			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "%s%s\n", checkbox, st.Name)
49: 		}
50: 		b.WriteString("\n")
51: 	}
52: 
53: 	if len(task.CustomFields) > 0 {
54: 		b.WriteString("## Reference: Custom Fields\n\n")
55: 		for _, cf := range task.CustomFields {
56: 			fmt.Fprintf(&b, "- **%s:** %s\n", cf.Name, cf.Value)
57: 		}
58: 		b.WriteString("\n")
59: 	}
60: 
61: 	return b.String()
62: }
63: 
64: // ScaffoldFilename generates a plan filename from a task name and date.
65: func ScaffoldFilename(name, date string) string {
66: 	slug := strings.ToLower(strings.TrimSpace(name))
67: 	slug = nonAlphanumeric.ReplaceAllString(slug, "-")
68: 	slug = strings.Trim(slug, "-")
69: 	return fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s.md", date, slug)
70: }
71: 
72: func isDone(status string) bool {
73: 	s := strings.ToLower(status)
74: 	return s == "done" || s == "complete" || s == "completed" || s == "closed"
75: }

146: // focusSlot constants for readability.
147: // Order matches tab layout: info (first), agent (second), diff (third).

156: // setFocusSlot updates which pane has focus and syncs visual state.
157: func (m *home) setFocusSlot(slot int) {
158: 	m.focusSlot = slot
159: 	m.nav.SetFocused(slot == slotNav)
160: 	m.menu.SetFocusSlot(slot)
161: 
162: 	// Center pane is focused when any of the 3 center tabs is active.
163: 	centerFocused := slot >= slotInfo && slot <= slotDiff
164: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocused(centerFocused)
165: 
166: 	// When focusing a center tab, switch the visible tab to match and track which tab is focused.
167: 	if centerFocused {
168: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(slot - slotInfo) // slotInfo=1 → InfoTab=0, etc.
169: 	}
170: 	// focusedTab always tracks activeTab so the gradient header is visible
171: 	// regardless of which pane has keyboard focus.
172: }

174: // nextFocusSlot cycles the visible center tab forward (info → agent → diff → info).
175: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav); only the
176: // displayed tab changes. This is called by Tab and →.
177: func (m *home) nextFocusSlot() {
178: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
179: 	case ui.InfoTab:
180: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
181: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
182: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
183: 	default: // ui.DiffTab — wraps to info
184: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
185: 	}
186: }
187:

189: // The sidebar always retains keyboard focus (focusSlot stays slotNav).
190: func (m *home) prevFocusSlot() {
191: 	switch m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() {
192: 	case ui.PreviewTab:
193: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
194: 	case ui.DiffTab:
195: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
196: 	default: // ui.InfoTab — wraps to diff
197: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.DiffTab)
198: 	}
199: }

201: // enterFocusMode enters focus/insert mode and starts the fast preview ticker.
202: // enterFocusMode reuses the existing previewTerminal if it is already attached to
203: // the selected instance — entering focus just toggles key forwarding to the same
204: // terminal. Only spawns a new terminal if none is attached yet (rare fallback).
205: func (m *home) enterFocusMode() tea.Cmd {
206: 	m.tabbedWindow.ClearDocumentMode()
207: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
208: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused {
209: 		return nil
210: 	}
211: 
212: 	// If previewTerminal is already attached to this instance, just enter focus mode.
213: 	if m.previewTerminal != nil && m.previewTerminalInstance == selected.Title {
214: 		m.state = stateFocusAgent
215: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
216: 		m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
217: 		return nil
218: 	}
219: 
220: 	// No terminal yet (shouldn't normally happen) — spawn one synchronously-ish.
221: 	cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
222: 	if cols < 10 {
223: 		cols = 80
224: 	}
225: 	if rows < 5 {
226: 		rows = 24
227: 	}
228: 	term, err := selected.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
229: 	if err != nil {
230: 		return m.handleError(err)
231: 	}
232: 	m.previewTerminal = term
233: 	m.previewTerminalInstance = selected.Title
234: 	m.state = stateFocusAgent
235: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetFocusMode(true)
236: 	m.menu.SetFocusMode(true)
237: 
238: 	return nil
239: }

250: // switchToTab changes the visible center tab without stealing focus from the sidebar.
251: // The sidebar (slotNav) always retains keyboard focus.
252: func (m *home) switchToTab(name keys.KeyName) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
253: 	var targetTab int
254: 	switch name {
255: 	case keys.KeyTabAgent:
256: 		targetTab = ui.PreviewTab
257: 	case keys.KeyTabDiff:
258: 		targetTab = ui.DiffTab
259: 	case keys.KeyTabInfo:
260: 		targetTab = ui.InfoTab
261: 	default:
262: 		return m, nil
263: 	}
264: 
265: 	if m.tabbedWindow.GetActiveTab() == targetTab {
266: 		return m, nil
267: 	}
268: 
269: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(targetTab)
270: 	return m, nil
271: }

292: // instanceChanged updates the preview pane, menu, and diff pane based on the selected instance.
293: // It returns a tea.Cmd when an async operation is needed (terminal spawn).
294: func (m *home) instanceChanged() tea.Cmd {
295: 	// selected may be nil
296: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
297: 
298: 	// Clear notification on the previously-viewed instance when the user
299: 	// navigates away. This prevents the item from jumping out of "attention"
300: 	// while the user is still looking at it.
301: 	if m.seenNotified != nil && m.seenNotified != selected {
302: 		m.seenNotified.Notified = false
303: 		m.seenNotified = nil
304: 		m.updateNavPanelStatus()
305: 	}
306: 	if selected != nil && selected.Notified {
307: 		m.seenNotified = selected
308: 	}
309: 
310: 	// Manage preview terminal lifecycle on selection change.
311: 	var spawnCmd tea.Cmd
312: 	if selected == nil || !selected.Started() || selected.Status == session.Paused || selected.Exited {
313: 		// No valid instance (or dead tmux session) — tear down terminal.
314: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
315: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
316: 		}
317: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
318: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
319: 	} else if selected.Title != m.previewTerminalInstance {
320: 		// Different instance selected — swap terminal.
321: 		if m.previewTerminal != nil {
322: 			m.previewTerminal.Close()
323: 		}
324: 		m.previewTerminal = nil
325: 		m.previewTerminalInstance = ""
326: 
327: 		cols, rows := m.tabbedWindow.GetPreviewSize()
328: 		if cols < 10 {
329: 			cols = 80
330: 		}
331: 		if rows < 5 {
332: 			rows = 24
333: 		}
334: 		capturedTitle := selected.Title
335: 		capturedInstance := selected
336: 		spawnCmd = func() tea.Msg {
337: 			term, err := capturedInstance.NewEmbeddedTerminalForInstance(cols, rows)
338: 			return previewTerminalReadyMsg{term: term, instanceTitle: capturedTitle, err: err}
339: 		}
340: 	}
341: 
342: 	m.tabbedWindow.UpdateDiff(selected)
343: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInstance(selected)
344: 	m.updateInfoPane()
345: 	// Update menu with current instance
346: 	m.menu.SetInstance(selected)
347: 
348: 	// Auto-switch tab based on selection type: plan header → info tab, instance → agent tab.
349: 	// Only auto-switch when the nav panel is focused to avoid hijacking explicit tab selection.
350: 	if m.focusSlot == slotNav {
351: 		if selected != nil {
352: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.PreviewTab)
353: 		} else if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {
354: 			m.tabbedWindow.SetActiveTab(ui.InfoTab)
355: 		}
356: 	}
357: 
358: 	// Collect async commands.
359: 	var cmds []tea.Cmd
360: 	if spawnCmd != nil {
361: 		cmds = append(cmds, spawnCmd)
362: 	}
363: 
364: 	if len(cmds) == 0 {
365: 		return nil
366: 	}
367: 	if len(cmds) == 1 {
368: 		return cmds[0]
369: 	}
370: 	return tea.Batch(cmds...)
371: }

374: // switches the preview to show its output behind an overlay dialog. The user
375: // can see the agent's terminal behind the overlay to understand context before
376: // responding. Returns a tea.Cmd if an async terminal spawn is needed.

385: // focusPlanInstanceForOverlay selects the best instance belonging to the given
386: // plan file, preferring running instances. This is used before showing overlay
387: // dialogs about plan lifecycle events (wave completion, etc.) so the user can
388: // see the agent output behind the overlay.
389: func (m *home) focusPlanInstanceForOverlay(planFile string) tea.Cmd {
390: 	var best *session.Instance
391: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
392: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
393: 			continue
394: 		}
395: 		if best == nil {
396: 			best = inst
397: 		}
398: 		// Prefer running instances over ready/paused ones.
399: 		if inst.Status == session.Running && best.Status != session.Running {
400: 			best = inst
401: 		}
402: 	}
403: 	return m.focusInstanceForOverlay(best)
404: }

406: func statusString(s session.Status) string {

412: 	case session.Loading:

416: 	default:

421: // updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader populates the info tab when a plan header is selected
422: // (no instance). Shows plan metadata and instance summary counts.
423: func (m *home) updateInfoPaneForPlanHeader() {
424: 	planFile := m.nav.GetSelectedPlanFile()
425: 	if planFile == "" || m.planState == nil {
426: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
427: 		return
428: 	}
429: 	entry, ok := m.planState.Entry(planFile)
430: 	if !ok {
431: 		m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(ui.InfoData{IsPlanHeaderSelected: true})
432: 		return
433: 	}
434: 	data := ui.InfoData{
435: 		IsPlanHeaderSelected: true,
436: 		PlanName:             planstate.DisplayName(planFile),
437: 		PlanStatus:           string(entry.Status),
438: 		PlanTopic:            entry.Topic,
439: 		PlanBranch:           entry.Branch,
440: 	}
441: 	if !entry.CreatedAt.IsZero() {
442: 		data.PlanCreated = entry.CreatedAt.Format("2006-01-02")
443: 	}
444: 	// Count instances belonging to this plan.
445: 	for _, inst := range m.nav.GetInstances() {
446: 		if inst.PlanFile != planFile {
447: 			continue
448: 		}
449: 		data.PlanInstanceCount++
450: 		switch {
451: 		case inst.Status == session.Running || inst.Status == session.Loading:
452: 			data.PlanRunningCount++
453: 		case inst.Status == session.Paused:
454: 			data.PlanPausedCount++
455: 		default:
456: 			data.PlanReadyCount++
457: 		}
458: 	}
459: 	// Include wave progress if an orchestrator exists for this plan.
460: 	if orch, ok := m.waveOrchestrators[planFile]; ok {
461: 		data.TotalWaves = orch.TotalWaves()
462: 		data.TotalTasks = orch.TotalTasks()
463: 		tasks := orch.CurrentWaveTasks()
464: 		data.WaveTasks = make([]ui.WaveTaskInfo, len(tasks))
465: 		for i, task := range tasks {
466: 			state := "pending"
467: 			if orch.IsTaskComplete(task.Number) {
468: 				state = "complete"
469: 			} else if orch.IsTaskFailed(task.Number) {
470: 				state = "failed"
471: 			} else if orch.IsTaskRunning(task.Number) {
472: 				state = "running"
473: 			}
474: 			data.WaveTasks[i] = ui.WaveTaskInfo{Number: task.Number, State: state}
475: 		}
476: 	}
477: 	m.tabbedWindow.SetInfoData(data)
478: }

482: 	selected := m.nav.GetSelectedInstance()
483: 	if selected == nil {

485: 		if m.nav.IsSelectedPlanHeader() {

507: 	}

510: 		if m.planState != nil {

514: 				data.PlanName = planstate.DisplayName(selected.PlanFile)
515: 				data.PlanDescription = entry.Description
516: 				data.PlanStatus = string(entry.Status)
517: 				data.PlanTopic = entry.Topic
